cards.front: What is the Brute Force Approach?
cards.back: A method of solving a problem by trying all possible solutions

cards.front: What is the Traveling Salesman Problem?
cards.back: A classic problem in combinatorial optimization and computer science, where the goal is to find the shortest possible tour that visits a set of cities and returns to the origin city
A method of solving a problem by trying all possible solutions, where every possible combination of inputs is considered

cards.front: What is the Big O notation for the Time Complexity of Brute Force Approach?
cards.back: O(n!), where n is the number of elements to be considered, since it involves trying all possible permutations

cards.front: What is the main disadvantage of the Brute Force Approach?
cards.back: It is extremely inefficient for large inputs, since it involves trying all possible solutions, resulting in exponential time complexity
